title: Add podman baseline test script
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

To validate Buildah, I'd put together a little script that does basic Buildah stuff that can be run on a newly installed vm with the latest Buildah kit to make sure it's OK and then I feel comfortable giving the kit karma.  This script is the podman variant.  It does not run during Travis CI.  It runs only after being dropped on a machine and then invoked with '/bin/bash -v test_podman_baseline.sh'

It's not terribly fancy nor does it hit every podman nook and cranny.  As time goes on we can add more commands and perhaps do better error handling.  At the moment you run the script then scroll up to check on errors or if you use the '-e' parameter, the script will stop on unexpected errors.

comments.body: Yep, @rhatdan is right, I prefer it to let it run and see if it trips over more than one error.  But I was thinking about it last night as I know others would rather it stop on error.  Perhaps I can make that a conditional to stop or not based on a passed in param.  Will think and play.

+1

Still some regressions on complex stuff.

William

On Wed, Feb 28, 2018 at 7:01 AM Tom Sweeney <notifications@github.com>

Sent from Gmail Mobile on iPhone
It had problems with the Docker piece, I think it was a VM issue, but haven't checked.  Was saving that and the error handling for my weekend entertainment.

I've rerun on a new VM, the Docker issue appears to have been on that particular VM as I've not been able to duplicate it.  I also added 'set -eu' as suggested by @mheon, but I've hid it behind a parameter so you can either stop on error or just slog through.
